title: Button-Controlled LED
subtitle: Light an LED only while a button is held down.

hints.content: INPUT_PULLUP means the pin reads HIGH when the button is NOT pressed, and LOW when it IS pressed.
Wire the LED through a resistor to pin 13 and GND, just like the blink mission — then use digitalRead(2) to decide HIGH or LOW.
void loop() { digitalWrite(13, digitalRead(2) == LOW ? HIGH : LOW); }

narrative.briefing: Robots react to the world through inputs, not just outputs. Wire up a push button and make an LED respond to it in real time.
narrative.context: digitalRead() returns the current voltage on a pin as HIGH or LOW. Combined with INPUT_PULLUP, a button press pulls the pin to LOW.
narrative.successMessage: Your LED responds to the button! You just built your first input-driven circuit.
